question_text: A permanent magnet has a north pole and a south pole. Two magnets are pushed towards each other with their north poles facing each other. Describe what you would observe and explain why this happens.
correct_answer: The magnets would repel (push away from) each other. This happens because like poles (both north poles) repel each other. Opposite poles attract, but like poles always repel.
explanation: This tests understanding of the fundamental rule of magnetic forces: like poles repel and opposite poles attract. Students should observe the repulsive force and explain it using the rule about pole interactions, which is core Year 8 magnetism knowledge.
